Role: Tech – Software Engineer : Tradenet Rebuild [GDPA]
Project Description:
This internship project focuses on building a brand-new, modern web platform designed for all traders in Singapore.

The system will serve as a unified, mobile-friendly web application that simplifies how traders access and interact with essential services, with a strong emphasis on usability, reliability, and scalability.

Interns will be involved from the ground up — from shaping the system architecture to implementing core features. The project adopts a modern full-stack approach, with a Kotlin-based backend powering APIs and business logic, and a React-based frontend delivering a responsive, accessible user experience across desktop and mobile devices.

In addition to the application itself, the team will also design and build a reusable component library, ensuring consistency, maintainability, and long-term extensibility across the product ecosystem. This is not a maintenance project — it is an opportunity to help establish a foundational service that will be used by real users nationwide.
Learning Outcomes from Project:
By the end of the internship, interns can expect to gain hands-on experience in:

1. Designing and building a production-grade full-stack web application from scratch

2. Developing backend services using Kotlin, including API design, data handling, and service integration

3. Building modern, mobile-friendly user interfaces with React, focusing on usability and performance

This internship is ideal for students who want more than just exposure — it offers a chance to own real features, learn modern technologies, and contribute meaningfully to a system that supports Singapore’s trading ecosystem.
Prerequisites:
Essential Requirements

1. Proficient in at least one programming language and comfortable writing clean, maintainable code

2. Basic understanding of web application fundamentals, including frontend–backend interaction, APIs, and HTTP concepts

3. Willingness to work across the stack and learn both backend (Kotlin) and frontend (React) technologies

4. Strong problem-solving skills, curiosity, and the ability to learn independently in a fast-moving engineering environment

Preferred (Good to Have)

1. Prior experience with Kotlin, Java, or JVM-based languages, and/or React or modern JavaScript frameworks

2. Familiarity with modern web development practices such as RESTful APIs, component-based UI design, or design systems

3. Exposure to cloud platforms, CI/CD pipelines, or container-based development will be an advantage

Role: Tech – Software Engineer : Query at Gov [GDPA]

QAG is is built on top of our in-house RAG-as-a-Service and LLM-as-a-Service platforms, and interns will have opportunities to work with, and contribute to, these backend services, including, but not limited to, data preparation, API integration, reliability improvements, observability, evaluation and performance tuning.

5. Interest in backend systems and AI applications (RAG/LLM concepts); prior exposure is helpful but not required.
